title: Violence amid PSG celebrations was not gang-driven, but acts by 'varied profiles'
subhead: Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League victory was followed by unrest in the French capital. The arrests and trials that followed present a different picture from the one hastily drawn by some politicians. With the World Cup underway, local authorities remain vigilant.

Killing of Lyhanna: What we know about the previous accusations against the suspect
subhead: While awaiting the findings of the inquiries by the national gendarmerie's internal affairs body and the Justice Ministry's oversight authority, Le Monde has compiled the established facts and the outstanding questions surrounding the background of the suspect in the 11-year-old girl's murder.

title: France prepares for another 'very intense and widespread' heat event, possibly over 40°C
subhead: The southern half of France is set to be hit first this weekend. Starting Tuesday, the heat is expected to worsen and move northward. Temperatures could reach or exceed 40°C, although some uncertainty remains.

title: The perfume industry's troubled waters, from Hormuz to climate change
subhead: The fragrance industry is feeling the full force of global upheavals, which are causing supply chain problems and putting pressure on prices. In response to rising risks, the sector is reorganizing and developing new tools to better anticipate crises.

title: NBA Finals: New York rediscovers passion for basketball and the Knicks
subhead: The New York Knicks have a chance to win the NBA championship for the first time since 1973 if they prevail on Saturday against Victor Wembanyama's San Antonio Spurs. Such a feat would mark a long-awaited reconciliation between New York and its storied franchise.

Josh O'Connor, star of Spielberg's 'Disclosure Day,' wants to 'cultivate his garden'
subhead: At 36, he is one of the most sought-after actors in both independent cinema and major Hollywood productions. In just a few years, the British artist, who is passionate about gardening, has put his delicate masculinity and subtle acting style to work for a wide range of characters, from a lovesick farmer to Prince Charles or a priest.

title: The RN's fascination with LFI's presidential election 'war machine'
subhead: Despite their political opposition, Marine Le Pen and senior figures in the far-right Rassemblement National party often praise the organization of the radical left LFI, particularly its ability to stay on message.

title: David Hockney, giant of British painting, dies at the age of 88
subhead: Internationally renowned, notably for his painting 'A Bigger Splash' (1967) and his series of pool paintings, the British artist, who lived in Normandy from 2019 to 2023, produced work that straddled pop art and hyperrealism, never hesitating to use new technologies as sources of inspiration. He died on June 11.
